kitchenWorkflowToggle.modeSimpleSummary: Simple — just Accept or Reject (recommended for busy restaurants)
kitchenWorkflowToggle.modeTrackingSummary: Tracking — kitchen updates each order through every stage
kitchenWorkflowToggle.simpleDescription: Kitchen sees only Accept and Reject. Orders move into 'In Progress' on accept and stay there until end-of-day. No status updates needed per order. Best for busy restaurants — same model GloriaFood uses.
kitchenWorkflowToggle.trackingDescription: Kitchen explicitly moves each order through Preparing → Ready → Complete. Customer gets a notification at each step. Slower workflow but the most precise status updates.
kitchenWorkflowToggle.trackingPoint3: Better for slower-paced or upscale restaurants
kitchenWorkflowToggle.switchModeNote: You can switch modes anytime. Orders already in progress keep their current status when you switch — only NEW orders follow the new mode.
kitchenWorkflowToggle.switchedToSimpleToast: Switched to Simple mode — kitchen just accepts orders
kitchenWorkflowToggle.switchedToTrackingToast: Switched to Tracking mode — kitchen updates each order through every stage
kitchenWorkflowToggle.printNodeStatusOn: ON — kitchen also shows PrintNode setup as a backup option
kitchenWorkflowToggle.printNodeEnabledToast: PrintNode backup enabled — kitchen now shows PrintNode setup option
kitchenWorkflowToggle.printNodeDisabledToast: PrintNode backup disabled — only direct WiFi/LAN printing shown
kitchenWorkflowToggle.printNodeFooterNote: Direct WiFi/LAN printing (via the Fee Free Kitchen native app on Android/iOS tablet) is the main printer connection — it auto-discovers your printer on the network, no PrintNode account or monthly fee needed. Enable PrintNode only as a backup if you can't install the native app or are running the kitchen on a Windows browser.

Every new location starts on its own FREE plan (100 orders/month, no card required) and chooses which add-ons and features it wants to subscribe to — Unlimited Orders, Marketplace, Driver Pool, Sales-Optimized Website, and more. Each location has its own hours, payment provider, and Stripe Connect account.

autopilotClient.pageSubtitle: Run your marketing on autopilot with automated email campaigns that bring customers back.
autopilotClient.masterToggleOnDesc: Autopilot is ON. Enabled campaigns below will fire automatically.
autopilotClient.masterToggleOffDesc: When OFF, no campaigns fire — even if individual toggles are enabled.
autopilotClient.step1Body: Set trigger rules, email templates, and optional coupon codes.
autopilotClient.step2Body: Toggle each campaign on and save your settings.
autopilotClient.step3Body: Customers receive emails based on their behavior — no manual work needed.
autopilotClient.segmentBody: Right now Autopilot sends the same email to everyone who matches a campaign trigger. Soon you'll be able to target specific groups — VIP regulars, lapsed customers, big spenders, dietary preferences — and tune the message for each. The campaigns below still run today; segmentation is purely additive when it lands.
autopilotClient.disabledTitle: Turn on master toggle to enable individual campaigns.
autopilotClient.masterToggleHint: Turn on the master toggle to enable individual campaigns.
autopilotClient.couponSectionHint: Use {coupon_section} in your email body to show the coupon.
autopilotClient.emailNotConfiguredBody: Set <emailServer>EMAIL_SERVER</emailServer> and <emailFrom>EMAIL_FROM</emailFrom> in your environment variables to enable sending. Campaign settings will be saved but emails won't be sent until configured.
autopilotClient.campaign_second_order_tagline: Win back customers after their first order
autopilotClient.campaign_second_order_description: Automatically emails a customer after their first completed order if they have not ordered again.
autopilotClient.campaign_second_order_triggerLabel: Send this many hours after first order:
autopilotClient.campaign_second_order_defaultSubject: Thanks for your order! Here's a little something for next time
autopilotClient.campaign_second_order_defaultBody:
Hi {customer_name},

Thank you so much for your recent order from {restaurant_name}! We hope you enjoyed it.

As a thank-you, here's a special offer just for you:

{coupon_section}

We'd love to see you back soon!

– The {restaurant_name} team
autopilotClient.campaign_cart_abandonment_tagline: Recover orders that didn't make it through checkout
autopilotClient.campaign_cart_abandonment_description: Sends a reminder to customers who added items to their cart and entered their email but didn't complete their order. Fires ~90 minutes after they go quiet.
autopilotClient.campaign_cart_abandonment_triggerLabel: Send this many hours after cart goes quiet:
autopilotClient.campaign_cart_abandonment_defaultBody:
Hi {customer_name},

You started an order at {restaurant_name} but didn't finish checking out. Your cart is still here — come back and pick up where you left off.

{coupon_section}

Tap below to complete your order.

– The {restaurant_name} team
autopilotClient.campaign_reengagement_tagline: Bring back customers who haven't ordered in a while
autopilotClient.campaign_reengagement_description: Sends a re-engagement email to customers who haven't placed an order for a set number of days.
autopilotClient.campaign_reengagement_triggerLabel: Send after this many days without an order:
autopilotClient.campaign_reengagement_defaultSubject: We miss you! Come back for something delicious
autopilotClient.campaign_reengagement_defaultBody:
Hi {customer_name},

It's been a while since your last order from {restaurant_name} and we miss you!

Come back and treat yourself — we have some amazing dishes waiting for you.

{coupon_section}

We hope to see you soon!

– The {restaurant_name} team

driverPool.headingDescription: Configure how your deliveries get dispatched. Mix your own drivers with ShipDay's third-party pool — or use one exclusively.

driverPool.sourceOwnDescription: Your in-house drivers handle every delivery. ShipDay is never invoked. Free.
driverPool.sourceShipdayDescription: Every delivery routes to ShipDay's third-party pool automatically.
driverPool.sourceBothDescription: Kitchen sees a picker on each delivery order: in-house or pool.

driverPool.feePassThroughDescription: Customer pays the entire ShipDay fee. You absorb nothing.
driverPool.feeFlatDescription: Customer pays a fixed fee no matter what ShipDay charges. You absorb the gap.
driverPool.flatFeeInputHint: flat delivery fee shown to customer
driverPool.feeTieredDescription: Charge less (or zero) when the order is bigger. e.g. free over $50.

registrarGuide.whereDoIAddRecords: Where do I add these records?
registrarGuide.godaddyStep0: Open GoDaddy → click your domain name in My Products
registrarGuide.godaddyStep1: Click the DNS tab (top of the page)
registrarGuide.godaddyStep2: Scroll to the Records section → click Add New Record
registrarGuide.godaddyStep3: Type: paste the Type column above (e.g. CNAME or A)
registrarGuide.godaddyStep4: Name: paste the Name column above (often @ for apex, or the subdomain like 'order')
registrarGuide.godaddyStep5: Value: paste the Value column above
registrarGuide.godaddyStep7: Click Save → repeat for every row above
registrarGuide.namecheapStep0: Open Namecheap → Domain List in the left sidebar
registrarGuide.namecheapStep1: Click Manage next to your domain
registrarGuide.namecheapStep2: Click the Advanced DNS tab (top of the page)
registrarGuide.namecheapStep3: Click Add New Record under Host Records
registrarGuide.namecheapStep4: Type: pick the matching value from the dropdown
registrarGuide.namecheapStep5: Host: paste the Name (use @ for apex)
registrarGuide.namecheapStep8: Click the green checkmark to save → repeat for each row
registrarGuide.cloudflareStep0: Open Cloudflare dashboard → click your domain
registrarGuide.cloudflareStep3: Type: pick from the dropdown (CNAME / A / etc)
registrarGuide.cloudflareStep4: Name: paste the Name column (use @ for apex)
registrarGuide.cloudflareStep5: Target / Content: paste the Value column
registrarGuide.cloudflareStep6: IMPORTANT: set Proxy status to DNS only (grey cloud), NOT Proxied (orange cloud) — Vercel needs to see the real records
registrarGuide.cloudflareStep7: Click Save → repeat for every row above
registrarGuide.googleStep0: Google Domains was acquired by Squarespace — open https://domains.squarespace.com/
registrarGuide.googleStep1: Click your domain → DNS settings in the left sidebar
registrarGuide.googleStep2: Scroll to Custom records → click Add record
registrarGuide.googleStep4: Host: paste the Name (@ for apex)
registrarGuide.googleStep7: Click Save → repeat for each row
registrarGuide.shopifyStep4: Type / Hostname / Target fields map directly to the table above
registrarGuide.otherStep0: Log into the website where you bought the domain (your registrar)
registrarGuide.otherStep1: Look for a section called DNS, DNS Management, Zone File, or Advanced DNS
registrarGuide.otherStep2: Find the option to Add a Record or Add Custom Record
registrarGuide.otherStep3: For each row in the table above, create one record:
registrarGuide.otherStep5: • Name / Host: paste the Name value (@ usually means apex)
registrarGuide.otherStep6: • Value / Target / Data: paste the Value column
registrarGuide.otherStep8: If your registrar isn't listed here and you get stuck, contact our support — we'll walk you through it

paymentProviders.pageSubtitle: Connect your Stripe account to accept card payments from your customers. Money lands directly in your Stripe balance — Fee Free Ordering takes 0% per order. You only pay Stripe's standard processing fee.
paymentProviders.stripeNotEnabledBody: The platform hasn't finished setting up Stripe. Contact support to enable online payments for your restaurant.
paymentProviders.noAddOnBody: Card payments are unlocked by the Online Payments add-on. Without it, even a connected Stripe account can't accept card charges from your customers — they'll get redirected to cash / pay-at-store only. You can still set up Stripe Connect now and the add-on later, but you won't accept cards until both are done.
paymentProviders.subscribedOneStepLeftTitle: You're subscribed to Online Payments — one step left
paymentProviders.subscribedOneStepLeftBody: Connect your Stripe account below to start accepting card payments. Onboarding takes about 5 minutes.
paymentProviders.cardOffTitle: Online card payment is currently OFF
paymentProviders.cardOffBody: You're subscribed to the Online Payments add-on, but customers can't pay by card yet — you haven't enabled it in Accepted Methods. Connecting Stripe alone won't change that.
paymentProviders.returnedFromStripeBody: We're syncing your account status. Refresh in a moment if it doesn't update.
paymentProviders.featureOnboardingBody: Sign in or create your Stripe account in a few minutes.
paymentProviders.featureNoCardDataTitle: No card data on our servers
paymentProviders.featureNoCardDataBody: Card details go straight to Stripe — we never see them.
paymentProviders.featureMoneyLandsBody: Stripe pays you directly. Fee Free Ordering takes 0% per order — you only pay Stripe's processing fee.
paymentProviders.stripeNeedsMoreInfo: Stripe still needs more info to enable charges. Click below to finish onboarding.
paymentProviders.stripeDisconnectConfirm: Disconnect your Stripe account? You won't be able to accept payments until you reconnect.
paymentProviders.ppErrorBothRequired: Both Client ID and Secret are required.
paymentProviders.ppDisconnectConfirm: Disconnect your PayPal account? Customers won't be able to pay with PayPal until you reconnect.
paymentProviders.ppSetupIntro: One-time setup — paste credentials from your PayPal Business account's REST app. We encrypt them at rest; PayPal money flows directly to your account, Fee Free Ordering takes 0% per order.
paymentProviders.ppShowInstructions: Don't know where to find these? Show step-by-step instructions
paymentProviders.ppInstructionsTitle: How to get your PayPal credentials (about 5 minutes)
paymentProviders.ppStep1: Make sure you have a PayPal Business account (not a personal one). Personal accounts can't take payments via the API. Upgrade or create one. It's free to open and there's nothing to pay monthly — PayPal only charges per transaction, same as their normal rates.
paymentProviders.ppStep2: Sign in to the PayPal Developer Dashboard with the SAME credentials as your Business account: developer.paypal.com → Apps & Credentials. You'll land on the Live tab by default — that's the one you want for real customer payments.
paymentProviders.ppStep3: Click "Create App". Give it a name like "Fee Free Ordering" so you remember what it's for. Choose "Merchant" when asked what type. Submit. PayPal will land you on the app's detail page.
paymentProviders.ppStep4: Near the top of the app page you'll see "Client ID" and "Secret". Click Show next to the Secret to reveal it, then copy both. Paste them into the fields below. Tip: the Client ID is the long alphanumeric string starting with . The Secret usually starts with .
paymentProviders.ppStep5: Pick the matching Environment below — pick Live for real customer payments, or Sandbox if you want to test with PayPal's fake-money accounts first. (If you grabbed the credentials from the developer dashboard's "Sandbox" tab, you MUST pick Sandbox — Live credentials won't work as Sandbox and vice versa.)
paymentProviders.ppStep6: Click "Verify & Connect PayPal". We'll test the credentials by asking PayPal for an OAuth token. If it works, the panel switches to "Connected" and customers can immediately pay with PayPal at checkout.
paymentProviders.ppSecurityNote: Security note: We encrypt your Client ID and Secret with AES-256-GCM before storing. They're never visible in your browser or any log file again after the initial save. If you ever rotate them in PayPal's dashboard, just come back here and paste the new ones.
paymentProviders.ppStuckNote: Stuck? Email support@feefreeordering.com with a screenshot of where you're stuck — we'll walk you through.
paymentProviders.envHint: Use Sandbox to test with PayPal's fake-money accounts. Switch to Live when ready to take real payments.
paymentProviders.secretHint: We encrypt this with AES-256-GCM before storing. It's never sent back to your browser after this.
paymentProviders.merchantEmailHint: Shown back to you here so you can tell at a glance which PayPal account is wired up.
paymentProviders.paypalNotInAcceptedMethods: PayPal is connected but not yet listed in Accepted Methods — customers won't see it as a payment option. Enable it here.

driverPoolLocked.heroDescription: Overflow delivery capacity when your own drivers are busy or you don't have any. ShipDay's third-party drivers pick up from you and deliver to your customer. You pay per delivery; you decide how much customers see.
driverPoolLocked.feature1Body: When your own driver is mid-route and a new delivery comes in, route it to ShipDay's pool. No more 'sorry we're not delivering right now'.
driverPoolLocked.feature2Body: Pass-through (customer pays full ShipDay fee), flat (you absorb the gap), or tiered (e.g. free over $50). Total control.
driverPoolLocked.feature3Body: Mix your own drivers with ShipDay. The kitchen sees a picker for each delivery: in-house or pool. Your call, every time.

marketplaceLocked.heading: Get on the Fee Free Marketplace
marketplaceLocked.subheading: List your restaurant on our public marketplace at /marketplace. No 30% commission, no extra fees for customers. Pick the plan that fits your volume — you can switch any time.
marketplaceLocked.taxNote: USD · CA tax by province · US/intl exempt
marketplaceLocked.paygFeatureNoSubscription: No subscription, opt out any time
marketplaceLocked.recommendationBody: Start with Pay-As-You-Go until you're consistently getting 60–70 marketplace orders per month. At that volume the Monthly plan ($199.99) starts saving you money vs. PAYG ($3/order × 70 = $210). Below 60 orders, PAYG is the cheaper choice and you can switch any time.
marketplaceLocked.compareBody: 30% of every order, forever. On a $700 sales day that's $210 — almost our entire monthly bill, gone in one day.
marketplaceLocked.featureLocalBody: Customers browsing our marketplace see your restaurant alongside other independents — never competing against 1000s of national chains.
marketplaceLocked.featureAutoListedBody: Subscribe and you're listed instantly. Your menu, your hours, your prices — we pull everything from your existing admin. Zero extra setup.
marketplaceLocked.featureDriverPoolBody: ShipDay third-party drivers when you need overflow capacity. Normally a $19.99/mo add-on — bundled free with Marketplace.
marketplaceLocked.footerNote: Already on a delivery aggregator? You can stay on both. Most restaurants who switch tell their best customers about the marketplace and steer them to it — same food, lower restaurant cost, lower customer cost.

terms.directOrdersFreeBody: We only charge when an order originates from our Marketplace app. Direct customers ordering at /order/your-slug or via your widget never trigger this fee.

paygOptInPage.monthlyUpsell: Prefer a flat predictable bill? <link>Subscribe to Marketplace Monthly ($199.99/mo)</link> — unlimited orders, Driver Pool included, charged upfront.

switch.pageSubtitle: You're currently on the Monthly Unlimited plan. Here's what happens when you switch.
switch.scheduledBody: Your Monthly plan continues until {switchDate}. On that date, Pay-As-You-Go kicks in automatically — $3 per marketplace order, capped at $249.99/month. Your listing stays live throughout; no gap in service.
switch.endOfCycle: the end of your current cycle
switch.driverPoolEndsHeading: Driver Pool ends with the Monthly plan
switch.driverPoolEndsBody: Pay-As-You-Go does not include the ShipDay Driver Pool. Without an active Driver Pool subscription on the switch date, you'll lose third-party driver dispatch — and Marketplace orders, which require drivers, can't be fulfilled. Subscribe to the standalone Driver Pool add-on ($9.99/mo) before the switch date to keep things running.
switch.timelineNowBody: We schedule your Monthly subscription to cancel at the end of the current cycle. You keep Monthly benefits (unlimited orders, Driver Pool bundled) until then.
switch.timelineSwitchDateBody: PAYG kicks in. From this date forward you're billed $3 per marketplace order (capped at $249.99/month). Your listing stays live — no gap in service.
switch.timelineAfterBody: Driver Pool is no longer included. If you rely on it, subscribe to the standalone Driver Pool add-on ($19.99/mo) before the switch date.
switch.timelineUndoBody: You can undo the switch any time before the cycle ends — just click the <q>Stay on Monthly</q> button that appears after you confirm.
switch.reminderNote: Reminder: PAYG starts billing $3 per marketplace order from the switch date forward. Direct orders (your own ordering page, widget, or branded mobile app) stay FREE forever — PAYG only charges for the marketplace channel.

paygOptInPage.switch.timelineSwitchDateBody: PAYG kicks in. From this date forward you're billed $3 per marketplace order (capped at $249.99/month). Your listing stays live — no gap in service.
paygOptInPage.switch.timelineAfterBody: Driver Pool is no longer included. If you rely on it, subscribe to the standalone Driver Pool add-on ($19.99/mo) before the switch date.
paygOptInPage.switch.timelineUndoBody: You can undo the switch any time before the cycle ends — just click the <q>Stay on Monthly</q> button that appears after you confirm.
paygOptInPage.switch.reminderNote: Reminder: PAYG starts billing $3 per marketplace order from the switch date forward. Direct orders (your own ordering page, widget, or branded mobile app) stay FREE forever — PAYG only charges for the marketplace channel.

legacyWidget.iframeWhereToPasteStep1: In Wix: drag in an "Embed HTML" element wherever you want the menu
legacyWidget.iframeWhereToPasteStep2: Paste the code below into it
legacyWidget.iframeWhereToPasteStep3: Resize the Wix element to roughly match the height above

legacyWidget.buttonLinkWhereToPasteStep1: In Wix: drag in an "Embed HTML" element wherever you want the button to appear (a section header, your "Order Online" page, anywhere)
legacyWidget.buttonLinkWhereToPasteStep2: Paste the code below into it
legacyWidget.buttonLinkWhereToPasteStep3: Wix shows a small white box — that's the button, click Preview or Publish to see it styled
legacyWidget.buttonLinkWhereToPasteNote: Click opens the full ordering experience in a new tab. Customer can browse, add to cart, and checkout there.
legacyWidget.buttonTextHint: What the button says. Default: "See MENU & Order" (the proven highest-clicked version).
legacyWidget.posBottomRightHint: Default. Always visible. ⚠️ May overlap with chat widgets (Tidio, Intercom, Wix Chat) which also default here — try bottom-left if you have a chat bubble.
legacyWidget.posBottomLeftHint: Avoids the bottom-right corner where most chat widgets live.
legacyWidget.posTopRightHint: High visibility on first scroll. Good when your hero section already has a CTA at the bottom.
legacyWidget.posTopLeftHint: Least common — usually conflicts with the site logo. Use only if other corners are taken.
legacyWidget.posInlineHint: Place the button anywhere on your page using a CSS selector.
legacyWidget.inlineModeWarningTitle: ⚠️ Inline mode requires a placeholder element
legacyWidget.inlineModeWarningBody:
For inline mode to work, your website needs an empty HTML element with a matching ID (e.g.
) placed exactly where you want the button to appear.
legacyWidget.inlineModeWarningFooter: If you're on Wix, Squarespace, or another no-code builder, this is usually hard or impossible. Those editors don't expose custom IDs. Use one of the Floating positions instead — they always work and you can pick any corner. If your inline target isn't found, the widget auto-falls-back to floating bottom-right so customers can still order.
legacyWidget.previewEmbeddedMenuDescription: The whole ordering UI renders right here, inline on your page.
legacyWidget.previewDescriptionIframe: The embed renders the menu inline — no popup, no overlay. Customers stay on your page while they order.
legacyWidget.previewDescriptionButtonLink: Clicking the button opens the full ordering page in a new browser tab. Customer browses, orders, checks out there — your original page stays open in their other tab.
legacyWidget.previewDescriptionPopup: Clicking the real button on your site opens a full-screen modal with your complete ordering UI — pickup/delivery toggle, menu categories, cart, checkout. Not previewed here.
legacyWidget.whereToPasteBody: The code goes into your website's site-wide custom code area — where Google Analytics or Facebook Pixel scripts go. NOT into an inline "Embed HTML" element on a page. Most website builders sandbox inline embeds in tiny iframes which prevents the ordering modal from opening full-size.
legacyWidget.platformPickerLabel: What platform is your website on?
legacyWidget.facebookHeading: Add a "Start Order" button to your Facebook Page
legacyWidget.facebookIntro: Turn your Facebook Page into an ordering surface — same pattern Oracle GloriaFood uses. Facebook lets every Page owner pin one action button at the top of the page; pick Start Order and paste your smart link below. Customers tap it and land straight on your menu, no app required.
legacyWidget.facebookSmartLinkHint: This is your public ordering URL. Safe to share anywhere — on Facebook, in Instagram bios, on business cards, in emails.
legacyWidget.facebookStep1: On your Facebook Page, click the ••• (three dots) menu → Add Action Button → pick Start Order.
legacyWidget.facebookStep2: Paste the smart link above into the Website link field.
legacyWidget.facebookStep3: Click Save. The button now appears at the top of your Facebook Page — every visitor can order in one tap.
legacyWidget.facebookSmartLinkChannels: The smart link also works inside Instagram bios, WhatsApp Business profiles, Google Business Profile, TikTok bios, and QR codes. Same link, every channel.
legacyWidget.wixStep1: Go to manage.wix.com and click your site.
legacyWidget.wixStep5: Paste the install code from above.
legacyWidget.wixStep6: Name: Fee Free Ordering Widget. Add to: All pages, load code once. Place in: Body — end.
legacyWidget.wixStep7: Click Apply, then publish your Wix site.
legacyWidget.wixWarn1: ⚠️ Do NOT paste this code into an "Embed HTML" element on a page. Wix sandboxes those in iframes and the ordering modal won't open full-size.
legacyWidget.wixWarn2: Don't see the button on your live site? If you have Wix Chat enabled it sits in the bottom-right corner and can cover our button. Switch the Button position above to "Floating, bottom-left" (or top-right) and re-copy the code. You don't need to re-paste into Wix — just edit the existing snippet to add data-position="bl".

legacyWidget.squarespaceStep2: Paste the install code into the Footer box.
legacyWidget.squarespaceStep3: Click Save. The widget loads on every page automatically.
legacyWidget.wordpressStep1: Easiest: install the free plugin Insert Headers and Footers (or WPCode) from Plugins → Add New.
legacyWidget.wordpressStep2: Open the plugin's settings. Paste the install code into the Footer / "Before " box.
legacyWidget.wordpressStep4: Alternative (developers): edit your theme's footer.php and paste the snippet just before .
legacyWidget.shopifyStep1: In your Shopify admin, go to Online Store → Themes.
legacyWidget.shopifyStep2: Click Actions → Edit code on your live theme.
legacyWidget.shopifyStep4: Find the closing tag near the bottom. Paste the install code on the line just above it.
legacyWidget.webflowStep1: In your Webflow project, click the Project Settings gear icon.
legacyWidget.webflowStep3: Paste the install code into the Footer Code box.

legacyWidget.godaddyStep2: ⚠️ GoDaddy sandboxes the HTML section in an iframe, similar to Wix. The button works but you'll need to size the section tall enough (~600px+) for customers to see the menu when they click it.
legacyWidget.godaddyStep3: Paste the install code into the HTML section.
legacyWidget.godaddyStep4: Resize the section to take a meaningful chunk of the page.
legacyWidget.godaddyWarn: If you have access to GoDaddy's site-wide settings or a paid plan with custom code support, paste this into the site footer instead — that's the cleaner install.
legacyWidget.htmlStep1: Open the HTML file for any page where you want the widget.
legacyWidget.htmlStep2: Find the closing tag at the bottom of the file.
legacyWidget.htmlStep3: Paste the install code on a new line just before .

phoneOrderingPage.pageSubtitle: An AI agent that answers your restaurant's phone 24/7, takes orders, and drops them straight into your kitchen display.
phoneOrderingPage.heroBody: Every call answered, every order taken, every time — even during your busiest rush. Our AI agent handles the phone so your staff can focus on the food. Orders flow directly into your kitchen display alongside online orders. Customers get the same fast, friendly experience whether they tap or talk.
phoneOrderingPage.featurePhoneBody: Get a local number for your restaurant — keep your existing line for emergencies, or forward it to ours during busy hours.
phoneOrderingPage.featureAiBody: The agent knows every item, every modifier, every variant on your menu. Pricing, allergens, prep time — all answered correctly, every time.
phoneOrderingPage.featureVoiceBody: Natural conversation, not a robotic phone tree. Customers won't realize they're talking to AI. Handles accents, background noise, and the inevitable "wait can you change that to".
phoneOrderingPage.featureKitchenBody: Phone orders land in your existing kitchen display alongside online orders. No new app, no new training — your team accepts them the same way.
phoneOrderingPage.statusBody1: We're heads-down building the foundation. The voice agent, the Twilio integration, the kitchen handoff — all in active development. We expect to roll this out to early-access restaurants soon after the main platform launches.
phoneOrderingPage.statusBody2: When it's ready you'll be able to subscribe to it from your billing page like any other add-on. Until then this page is just a heads-up that it's coming.

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before signing up.

items.q: Do you charge a commission on orders?
items.a: No. We charge a flat monthly subscription. You keep 100% of every order.
Most restaurants are taking orders within 30 minutes. Import your menu from PDF or add items manually.

items.q: Do you support pickup AND delivery?
items.a: Yes. You configure each service independently with its own ETA, fees, and minimum order.
Connect your own Stripe account directly. We never touch the funds — they go straight to your bank.

items.q: Can I use a thermal receipt printer?
items.a: Yes. We integrate with PrintNode for ESC/POS thermal printers (Star, Epson, etc.).
Yes. The reservation system supports table-by-table management, pre-orders, and optional deposits.
English, French, Spanish, Italian, and Portuguese — across customer ordering, kitchen display, admin panel, and emails.
Yes. Percent-off, fixed discounts, free delivery, BOGO, scheduled promotions, and one-off coupon codes.

Yes. The core ordering platform is free forever — no credit card, no trial period, no expiration. The FREE plan includes up to 100 orders per month. Past that, you can either upgrade to FREE Unlimited Orders ($14.99/month) or subscribe to any paid add-on. You only pay for the extras you actually want.

marketplace.pitchBody: Every restaurant here pays a flat monthly subscription — never a 30% commission. Menu prices are the menu prices. No service fees. No surge. No upsells. Just food.
marketplace.nativeAppsBody: The full-fat native apps are in the pipeline. In the meantime, tap your browser’s share menu and choose “Add to Home Screen” — you’ll get the marketplace as a real app icon, splash screen and full-screen experience. No App Store required.
